title: Helianthemum ledifolium
intro: Helianthemum ledifolium, commonly known as rockrose, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, offering potential health benefits in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Helianthemum ledifolium include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as diterpenes and phenolic acids. In herbal medicine, Helianthemum ledifolium is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and soothe skin irritations.
taxonomy: Helianthemum ledifolium is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is native to Europe and western Asia. The genus Helianthemum is commonly known as rockrose. The plant is a low-growing shrub with yellow flowers. It is often used as an ornamental in gardens due to its attractive flowers and ability to thrive in poor soil.
uses: Helianthemum ledifolium, also known as rockrose, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's extracts have been reported to exhibit wound-healing and antimicrobial activities, making it a potential treatment for skin conditions and infections. In addition, Helianthemum ledifolium has been used to treat respiratory issues, such as coughs and bronchitis, due to its expectorant properties. The plant's leaves and flowers are often used in folk remedies to treat various ailments, including fever, sore throats, and digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Helianthemum ledifolium and its efficacy in treating human diseases.

side_effects: Helianthemum ledifolium, also known as rockrose, is not commonly associated with severe side effects when used in traditional medicine. However, its use can cause minor issues such as skin irritation or allergic reactions in some individuals. People who are sensitive to plant-based compounds may experience stomach upset, nausea, or headaches after consuming it. In rare cases, Helianthemum ledifolium extracts may interact with certain medications, leading to adverse reactions.
